name: dram-backed-weights

fit_constraints.bram_formula: on-chip BRAM stores only prefetch_fifo_depth*64 bits plus optional active weight tile and bias cache; full weights remain in external memory.
fit_constraints.dram_bandwidth_assumption: 64-bit AXI read data at one beat per cycle when weights_arvalid/weights_arready and weights_rvalid/weights_rready sustain without bubbles.
buffer_sizing_rules: prefetch FIFO depth must cover worst-case MAC consumption burst
ready_in must stay low until pass-0 weights are prefetched, because latency is measured from first accepted valid_in
while computing OC pass N, prefetch OC pass N+1 into a separate cache/FIFO when needed to preserve exact latency
weights_araddr must address the serialized weight stream in byte order

protocol_rules: Do not store the full OC*K_TOTAL weight tensor on chip.
Pass window size is MP*K_TOTAL INT8 weights; pass_base_addr = oc_pass * MP * K_TOTAL bytes.
AXI rdata is 64 bits, little-endian packed as eight INT8 weights per beat.
The deterministic testbench services AXI reads from weights_path; generated RTL must use the AXI handshake rather than $readmemh for full weights.
